Skip to content
TES - Tilly Engineer Skills

Contents

  • 00Overview
  • 01Quick install
  • 02Command triggers
  • 03Routes and project states
  • 04Safety boundaries
  • 05Cortex and MCP
  • 06Field Reports
  • 07Obsidian
  • 08Final report
  • 09Rollback

User manual

Install, audit, update, and roll back TES without losing project control.

This manual is for users who already care about local evidence. It explains the safe path, the expected reports, the write boundaries, and the recovery path.

00

Overview

Tilly Engineer Skills installs a governance mesh for coding agents. Codex, Claude Code, and Cursor route through adapter-specific surfaces while project truth lives in versioned Markdown.

The normal user interface is an intent typed in the agent window, not a shell command. Type /tes-init to install, /tes-update to update, or /tes-open-obsidian to open the optional Markdown workbench.

Existing AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, CURSOR.md, and Cursor rules are first backed up under .tes/bk/<timestamp>/. TES then applies a clean runtime and recovers useful local semantics into docs/agents/**.

01

Quick install

Start in the agent window. The installer should inspect Git state, create rollback evidence, and report before you approve commit or push.

  1. Open the target project in Codex, Claude Code, or Cursor.
  2. Type /tes-init for first setup, or /tes-update if TES is already installed.
  3. Let Step Zero finish before approving edits. It records the baseline and checks required capabilities.
  4. Choose a route when prompted: current, codex, claude, cursor, all, mcp, or audit.
  5. Read the final report before asking for commit or push.

The installer stages bundles under .tes/setup/<version>/. Those files are staging cache, not project knowledge to commit.

02

Command triggers

Triggers are intent text for the active agent. They are not shell commands.

IntentUse forWrite behavior
/tes-initInstall, recertify, or initialize project context.May write TES runtime files and docs/agents/** after gates pass.
/tes-update / /tes:updateUpdate an existing mesh through tes_update.py.Starts with helper-only Layer Zero, then adapter-config only if gates pass.
/tes-alignDeepen project context into state, gates, glossary, and decisions.Writes operating mesh files under docs/agents/**.
/tes-cortexInspect, recall, audit, learn from, or reflect into Cortex.Memory writes require explicit authorization and evidence.
/tes-mcpActivate or verify read-only Cortex MCP.May write project-scoped MCP config and TES helpers.
/tes-field-reportsInspect, drain, disable, or enable sanitized reports.May update .tes/field-reports/** and the local pre-push hook.

Full trigger contract: COMMAND-TRIGGERS.md.

03

Routes and project states

The route decides which runtime surfaces move. The project state decides whether the run installs, updates, audits, or recovers.

RouteWhen to use it
currentUpdate the detected runtime and its read-only Cortex MCP config.
codexPrepare AGENTS.md, Codex skills, plugin metadata, and .codex/config.toml.
claudePrepare CLAUDE.md, .claude/skills/**, plugin skills, and .mcp.json.
cursorPrepare .cursor/rules/** and .cursor/mcp.json.
allPrepare all supported runtime surfaces.
auditInspect without modifying files.

Project states are new, existing, and meshed. Existing instructions are backed up and useful semantics are recovered instead of discarded.

04

Safety boundaries

TES is local and inspectable by default. It refuses broad release, remote, and marketplace behavior unless explicitly authorized.

  • No push, publish, tag, amend, remote change, dependency install, or marketplace action by default.
  • No .obsidian/** writes. Obsidian is optional viewing over Markdown.
  • No code, diffs, prompts, file contents, secrets, personal data, absolute paths, raw stack traces, branch names, or remote URLs in Field Reports.
  • The Root context gate uses root_context.py to inspect AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .cursor/rules/**, and docs/agents/** before overwrite or recovery.

Update checks compare installed and cloud versions, helper contract parity, runtime_trigger_status, recommended_route, recommended_update_scope, legacy_retirement_required, and self_test_mode. A clean no-op reports update_available=False, recommended_update_scope=none, helper_contract_status=PASS, and a final Field Reports tes_update event when reporting is enabled. STALE_HELPERS blocks adapter-config until helper repair is complete.

05

Cortex and MCP

Cortex is filesystem-first memory. Markdown is the contract; SQLite recall and MCP tools are derived access paths.

Cortex lives under docs/agents/cortex/**. Every durable claim needs evidence. Agents may propose memory, but writes require explicit authorization.

Cortex MCP is read-only and project-scoped. It can verify, audit, recall, read cells, plan absorbs, plan curation, and reflect. It cannot write to memory.

06

Field Reports

Field Reports help TES learn from real installations without exporting project content.

Reports capture sanitized operational facts and drain only during git push when GitHub CLI, authentication, and network are available. If any link is missing, nothing leaves the machine and push proceeds normally.

/tes:field-reports:disable
/tes:field-reports:enable
07

Obsidian

Obsidian is an optional visual workbench over Markdown. It is not a runtime dependency and not proof by itself.

  • Open docs/agents/ as the vault for the full mesh.
  • Open docs/agents/cortex/ alone for focused memory work.
  • Keep Markdown links readable outside Obsidian and avoid Obsidian-only proof.
08

Final report

The final report is the user-facing contract. Read it before approving commit or push.

It records status, source snapshot freshness, source_package_commit, source_remote_head, source_freshness, STALE_SOURCE, Current public bundles, the installed helper set, Field Reports state, root context gate, changed files, rollback summary, and whether the run was certified against the recorded snapshot.

Important statuses include RECOVERED, NEEDS_REVIEW, BLOCKED, DEGRADED, PASS, and STALE_SOURCE. A backup under .tes/bk/<timestamp> is rollback evidence, not a new TES surface.

09

Rollback

Rollback should be boring. TES tells you the baseline and the installer-created files so you can undo the run with Git.

git reset --hard <baseline-head>
git clean -fd -- .tes/setup/<version>

Do not reset blindly if unrelated user work exists. Ask the agent to explain the changed-file inventory and the clean backup first.

Manual de usuario

Instala, audita, actualiza y revierte TES sin perder control del proyecto.

Este manual es para usuarios que ya valoran la evidencia local. Explica el camino seguro, los informes esperados, los limites de escritura y la recuperacion.

00

Vista general

Tilly Engineer Skills instala un mesh de gobernanza para agentes de codigo. Codex, Claude Code y Cursor pasan por superficies especificas mientras la verdad del proyecto vive en Markdown versionado.

La interfaz normal es una intencion escrita en la ventana del agente, no un comando de shell. Usa /tes-init para instalar, /tes-update para actualizar o /tes-open-obsidian para abrir el workbench Markdown opcional.

Los archivos AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, CURSOR.md y reglas de Cursor existentes se respaldan primero en .tes/bk/<timestamp>/. TES aplica un runtime limpio y recupera semantica local util en docs/agents/**.

01

Instalacion rapida

Empieza en la ventana del agente. El instalador debe inspeccionar Git, crear evidencia de rollback y reportar antes de que apruebes commit o push.

  1. Abre el proyecto en Codex, Claude Code o Cursor.
  2. Escribe /tes-init para la primera instalacion, o /tes-update si TES ya esta instalado.
  3. Deja que Step Zero termine antes de aprobar ediciones. Registra el baseline y verifica capacidades.
  4. Elige ruta cuando se pida: current, codex, claude, cursor, all, mcp o audit.
  5. Lee el reporte final antes de pedir commit o push.

El instalador prepara bundles en .tes/setup/<version>/. Esos archivos son cache de staging, no conocimiento del proyecto para commitear.

02

Gatillos de comando

Los gatillos son texto de intencion para el agente activo. No son comandos de shell.

IntencionUsoEscritura
/tes-initInstalar, recertificar o inicializar contexto.Puede escribir runtime TES y docs/agents/** despues de gates.
/tes-update / /tes:updateActualizar un mesh existente mediante tes_update.py.Empieza con helper-only Layer Zero y luego adapter-config si pasan gates.
/tes-alignProfundizar estado, gates, glosario y decisiones.Escribe el mesh operativo en docs/agents/**.
/tes-cortexInspeccionar, recordar, auditar, aprender o reflexionar en Cortex.Escrituras de memoria requieren autorizacion y evidencia.
/tes-mcpActivar o verificar Cortex MCP read-only.Puede escribir config MCP con escopo de proyecto y helpers TES.
/tes-field-reportsInspeccionar, drenar, desactivar o activar reportes sanitizados.Puede actualizar .tes/field-reports/** y el hook pre-push local.

Contrato completo: COMMAND-TRIGGERS.md.

03

Rutas y estados del proyecto

La ruta decide que superficies se mueven. El estado decide si el run instala, actualiza, audita o recupera.

RutaCuando usarla
currentActualizar el runtime detectado y su config Cortex MCP read-only.
codexPreparar AGENTS.md, skills Codex, plugin metadata y .codex/config.toml.
claudePreparar CLAUDE.md, .claude/skills/**, plugin skills y .mcp.json.
cursorPreparar .cursor/rules/** y .cursor/mcp.json.
allPreparar todas las superficies soportadas.
auditInspeccionar sin modificar archivos.

Los estados son new, existing y meshed. Instrucciones existentes se respaldan y la semantica util se recupera, no se descarta.

04

Limites de seguridad

TES es local e inspeccionable por defecto. Rechaza release, remotes y marketplace salvo autorizacion explicita.

  • Sin push, publish, tag, amend, cambio de remote, instalacion de dependencias o marketplace por defecto.
  • Sin escrituras en .obsidian/**. Obsidian es vista opcional sobre Markdown.
  • Sin codigo, diffs, prompts, contenido de archivos, secretos, datos personales, paths absolutos, stack traces crudos, ramas o remotes en Field Reports.
  • El Root context gate usa root_context.py para inspeccionar AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .cursor/rules/** y docs/agents/** antes de sobrescribir o recuperar.

El update compara installed and cloud versions, helper contract parity, runtime_trigger_status, recommended_route, recommended_update_scope, legacy_retirement_required y self_test_mode. Un no-op limpio reporta update_available=False, recommended_update_scope=none, helper_contract_status=PASS y el evento final Field Reports tes_update si esta habilitado. STALE_HELPERS bloquea adapter-config hasta reparar helpers.

05

Cortex y MCP

Cortex es memoria filesystem-first. Markdown es el contrato; recall SQLite y tools MCP son accesos derivados.

Cortex vive en docs/agents/cortex/**. Todo claim durable necesita evidencia. Los agentes pueden proponer memoria, pero las escrituras requieren autorizacion explicita.

Cortex MCP es read-only y con escopo de proyecto. Puede verificar, auditar, recordar, leer cells, planear absorbs, planear curation y reflect. No puede escribir memoria.

06

Field Reports

Field Reports ayuda a TES a aprender de instalaciones reales sin exportar contenido del proyecto.

Los reportes capturan hechos operativos sanitizados y drenan solo durante git push cuando GitHub CLI, autenticacion y red estan disponibles. Si falta algo, nada sale de la maquina y el push continua.

/tes:field-reports:disable
/tes:field-reports:enable
07

Obsidian

Obsidian es un workbench visual opcional sobre Markdown. No es dependencia de runtime ni prueba por si mismo.

  • Abre docs/agents/ como vault para el mesh completo.
  • Abre docs/agents/cortex/ para trabajo de memoria enfocado.
  • Mantiene links Markdown legibles fuera de Obsidian y evita prueba solo-Obsidian.
08

Reporte final

El reporte final es el contrato visible para el usuario. Leelo antes de aprobar commit o push.

Registra estado, source snapshot freshness, source_package_commit, source_remote_head, source_freshness, STALE_SOURCE, Current public bundles, installed helper set, Field Reports state, root context gate, archivos cambiados, rollback summary y si el run fue certified against the recorded snapshot.

Estados importantes: RECOVERED, NEEDS_REVIEW, BLOCKED, DEGRADED, PASS y STALE_SOURCE. Un backup en .tes/bk/<timestamp> es evidencia de rollback, no una nueva superficie TES.

09

Rollback

Rollback debe ser aburrido. TES informa el baseline y los archivos creados por el instalador para que puedas deshacer con Git.

git reset --hard <baseline-head>
git clean -fd -- .tes/setup/<version>

No hagas reset a ciegas si existe trabajo no relacionado. Pide al agente el inventario de cambios y el clean backup primero.

Manual do usuario

Instale, audite, atualize e reverta TES sem perder controle do projeto.

Este manual e para usuarios que ja valorizam evidencia local. Ele explica o caminho seguro, os relatorios esperados, os limites de escrita e a recuperacao.

00

Visao geral

Tilly Engineer Skills instala uma malha de governanca para agentes de codigo. Codex, Claude Code e Cursor passam por superficies especificas enquanto a verdade do projeto vive em Markdown versionado.

A interface normal e uma intencao digitada na janela do agente, nao um comando de shell. Use /tes-init para instalar, /tes-update para atualizar ou /tes-open-obsidian para abrir a bancada Markdown opcional.

Arquivos existentes como AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, CURSOR.md e regras do Cursor sao primeiro salvos em .tes/bk/<timestamp>/. TES aplica um runtime limpo e recupera semântica útil em docs/agents/**.

01

Instalacao rapida

Comece na janela do agente. O instalador deve inspecionar Git, criar evidencia de rollback e reportar antes de voce aprovar commit ou push.

  1. Abra o projeto alvo no Codex, Claude Code ou Cursor.
  2. Digite /tes-init para a primeira instalacao, ou /tes-update se TES ja estiver instalado.
  3. Deixe o Step Zero terminar antes de aprovar edicoes. Ele registra baseline e verifica capacidades.
  4. Escolha uma rota quando solicitado: current, codex, claude, cursor, all, mcp ou audit.
  5. Leia o relatorio final antes de pedir commit ou push.

O instalador prepara bundles em .tes/setup/<version>/. Esses arquivos sao cache de staging, nao conhecimento do projeto para commit.

02

Gatilhos de comando

Gatilhos sao texto de intencao para o agente ativo. Nao sao comandos de shell.

IntencaoUsoEscrita
/tes-initInstalar, recertificar ou inicializar contexto.Pode escrever runtime TES e docs/agents/** depois dos gates.
/tes-update / /tes:updateAtualizar um mesh existente por tes_update.py.Comeca com helper-only Layer Zero e depois adapter-config se gates passarem.
/tes-alignAprofundar estado, gates, glossario e decisoes.Escreve a malha operacional em docs/agents/**.
/tes-cortexInspecionar, lembrar, auditar, aprender ou refletir no Cortex.Escritas de memoria exigem autorizacao explicita e evidencia.
/tes-mcpAtivar ou verificar Cortex MCP somente leitura.Pode escrever config MCP com escopo de projeto e helpers TES.
/tes-field-reportsInspecionar, drenar, desativar ou ativar relatorios sanitizados.Pode atualizar .tes/field-reports/** e o hook pre-push local.

Contrato completo: COMMAND-TRIGGERS.md.

03

Rotas e estados do projeto

A rota decide quais superficies se movem. O estado decide se o run instala, atualiza, audita ou recupera.

RotaQuando usar
currentAtualizar o runtime detectado e sua config Cortex MCP somente leitura.
codexPreparar AGENTS.md, skills Codex, plugin metadata e .codex/config.toml.
claudePreparar CLAUDE.md, .claude/skills/**, plugin skills e .mcp.json.
cursorPreparar .cursor/rules/** e .cursor/mcp.json.
allPreparar todas as superficies suportadas.
auditInspecionar sem modificar arquivos.

Os estados sao new, existing e meshed. Instrucoes existentes sao salvas em backup e a semântica útil e recuperada, nao descartada.

04

Limites de seguranca

TES e local e inspecionavel por padrao. Ela recusa release, remotes e marketplace sem autorizacao explicita.

  • Sem push, publish, tag, amend, mudanca de remote, instalacao de dependencias ou marketplace por padrao.
  • Sem escritas em .obsidian/**. Obsidian e visualizacao opcional sobre Markdown.
  • Sem codigo, diffs, prompts, conteudo de arquivos, segredos, dados pessoais, paths absolutos, stack traces crus, nomes de branch ou remotes em Field Reports.
  • O gate de contexto raiz usa root_context.py para inspecionar AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .cursor/rules/** e docs/agents/** antes de sobrescrever ou recuperar.

O update compara versão instalada e versão na nuvem, paridade de contrato dos helpers, runtime_trigger_status, recommended_route, recommended_update_scope, legacy_retirement_required e self_test_mode. Um no-op limpo reporta update_available=False, recommended_update_scope=none, helper_contract_status=PASS e o evento final Field Reports tes_update se reports estiverem ativos. STALE_HELPERS bloqueia adapter-config ate reparar helpers.

05

Cortex e MCP

Cortex e memoria filesystem-first. Markdown e o contrato; recall SQLite e tools MCP sao acessos derivados.

Cortex vive em docs/agents/cortex/**. Todo claim duravel precisa de evidencia. Agentes podem propor memoria, mas escritas exigem autorizacao explicita.

Cortex MCP e somente leitura e com escopo de projeto. Pode verificar, auditar, lembrar, ler cells, planejar absorbs, planejar curadoria e refletir. Nao pode escrever memoria.

06

Field Reports

Field Reports ajuda TES a aprender com instalacoes reais sem exportar conteudo do projeto.

Os relatorios capturam fatos operacionais sanitizados e drenam apenas durante git push quando GitHub CLI, autenticacao e rede estao disponiveis. Se faltar qualquer elo, nada sai da maquina e o push continua.

/tes:field-reports:disable
/tes:field-reports:enable
07

Obsidian

Obsidian e uma bancada visual opcional sobre Markdown. Nao e dependencia de runtime nem prova por si so.

  • Abra docs/agents/ como vault para a malha completa.
  • Abra docs/agents/cortex/ para trabalho focado de memoria.
  • Mantenha links Markdown legiveis fora do Obsidian e evite prova que so existe no Obsidian.
08

Relatorio final

O relatorio final e o contrato visivel para o usuario. Leia antes de aprovar commit ou push.

Ele registra status, frescor do snapshot fonte, source_package_commit, source_remote_head, source_freshness, STALE_SOURCE, Current public bundles, helper set instalado, estado do Field Reports, gate de contexto raiz, arquivos alterados, resumo de rollback e se o run foi certificado contra o snapshot registrado.

Estados importantes incluem RECOVERED, NEEDS_REVIEW, BLOCKED, DEGRADED, PASS e STALE_SOURCE. Um backup em .tes/bk/<timestamp> e evidencia de rollback, nao uma nova superficie TES.

09

Rollback

Rollback deve ser entediante. TES informa o baseline e os arquivos criados pelo instalador para voce desfazer com Git.

git reset --hard <baseline-head>
git clean -fd -- .tes/setup/<version>

Nao faca reset as cegas se houver trabalho de usuario nao relacionado. Peca ao agente o inventario de arquivos alterados e o clean backup primeiro.

v0.3.82 - Generated from JSON + YAML i18n sources. GitHub
